cases.input: We decided to standardize on Postgres for all new services because order-history queries need multi-table joins that a document store handles poorly.
Learned the hard way that retrying a flaky third-party API immediately just hammers it harder; exponential backoff with jitter fixed it.
Reusable pattern: wrap every flaky outbound network call in exponential backoff with jitter, not just the ones that already broke once.
Gotcha: comparing raw timestamps across two servers to order events broke in production because of clock skew — use a logical clock instead.
Decision: switched the frontend build tool from webpack to vite because hot-module-reload times had become painful as the codebase grew.
Fixed a typo in the README.
Ran the test suite again, still green, nothing changed.
